data.content:
[MB100](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB100)Studies in Molecular Biology:Presents students not majoring in molecular biology with inquiries into contemporary issues and phenomena in the biological sciences. Activities include lectures, readings, discussions, and laboratory or field experiences. May not be counted toward a...
[MB101](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB101)Genetics and Society A non-majors course covering the basic principles of classical and molecular genetics, and discussions concerning the impact of genetics on biological research, health care, ancestry, the legal system, and society. Course includes a laboratory compon...
[MB102](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB102)Viruses: The Biology of Epidemics No Description Set
[MB103](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB103)Epidemic & Infectious Disease Outbreaks of infectious disease have had profound impacts on human societies, and continue to impact communities in the present day. The course includes readings that situate selected epidemics in their social contexts, and explores the biological as...
[MB112](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB112)Investigations in Molecular Biology Introduces laboratory techniques and data analysis in molecular biology. Fundamentals of cell biology and microbiology.
[MB131](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB131)Introduction to Molecular and Cellular Biology Structures and functions of biomacromolecules, organelles, and cells and the flow of energy and information within and among them. Provides an introduction to molecular and cellular biology, with emphasis on how to address questions experimentally.
[MB199](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB199)Research Ethics in the Sciences Scientists today are faced with difficult choices due to reduced funding for research and higher expectations of research productivity. This pressure can lead to fraudulent behavior. Training in the responsible conduct of research is essential for st...
[MB201](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB201)Laboratory in Molecular and Cellular Biology and Genetics Laboratory techniques common in contemporary research in genetics, cell biology, and molecular biology. Introduction to research ethics.
[MB209](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB209)Introduction to Mentored Research in Molecular Biology Mentored research projects based on laboratory investigation.
[MB210](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB210)Introductory Special Topics in Molecular Biology Special topics not offered on a regular basis. Lecture/Discussion.
[MB230](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB230)Human Evolution Examines the fossil and genetic evidence for human evolution. Using the fossil record of early primate evolution as a foundation, the emergence of early hominins and their descendants is investigated. Human adaptations and hypotheses regarding the se...
[MB231](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB231)Genetics Nature, transmission and expression of hereditary information; lecture and discussion will include principles of molecular and transmission genetics with an emphasis on contemporary molecular genetic research.
[MB244](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB244)Scanning Electron Microscopy Basic principles of electron optical systems, image formation, specimen preparation, and photography. Individual instruction in the care, use and operation of the instrument as a research tool is emphasized. in the natural sciences.
[MB256](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB256)Mathematical Models in Molecular Biology An introduction to selected quantitative models drawn from ecology, genetics, and physiology. For each model the course includes an investigation of the mathematical methods used, an evaluation of the model, and some elementary simulation techniques....
[MB301](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB301)Special Topics in Molecular Biology Special topics not offered on a regular basis. Lecture/discussion.
[MB302](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB302)Independent Study in Molecular Biology Primary literature-intensive investigation of a selected topic in molecular biology.
[MB302.1](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB302.1)Independent Study in Molecular Biology Primary literature-intensive investigation of a selected topic in molecular biology.
[MB302.2](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB302.2)Independent Study in Molecular Biology:Primary literature-intensive investigation of a selected topic in molecular biology.
[MB310](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB310)Advanced Cell Biology Study of select processes that occur in eukaryotic cells such as cytoskeletal dynamics, membrane transport, protein targeting, cell-cell communication, and regulation of cell division and death. Heavy emphasis on how questions in cell biology are add...
[MB315](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB315)Genomics This course provides an introduction to how genome scale data are collected, analysed, and interpreted. A variety of applications for the use of genomic data are presented, and students have the opportunity to carry out a research project using bioin...
[MB321](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB321)Microbiology: Cells, Molecules, and Infection Bacteria, viruses, and eukaryotic microbes with emphasis on infectious agents that cause significant human disease. Laboratory work on cultivation and identification of microbes. Satisfies most pre-health requirements for a microbiology course with l...
[MB325](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB325)Molecular and Cellular Immunology Genetic, molecular, cellular, and developmental aspects of the immune system central to generation and regulation of immune responses. Through lecture, primary literature discussion, and case studies, this course explores individual components and co...
[MB335](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB335)Molecular Neurobiology This class will extend students’ understanding of genetics and molecular/cellular biology to the nervous system. Using case studies of sensory systems and neurological disorders, we will explore topics like ion channels and electrical signaling, syna...
[MB341](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB341)Special Topics in Molecular Biology Special topics in Biology not offered on a regular basis.
[MB345](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB345)Transmission Electron Microscopy Basic principles of electron optical systems, image formation, specimen preparation, ultramicrotomy, and photography. Individual instruction in the care, use, and operation of the instrument as a research tool is emphasized.
[MB350](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB350)Special Topics in Laboratory Research in Molecular Biology Special topics not offered on a regular basis. Laboratory intensive
[MB355](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB355)Laboratory in Advanced Genetics Laboratory-intensive course that covers the classical transmission genetics and modern molecular genetics techniques. Course emphasizes experimental design, techniques, data gathering, data analysis, and technical science writing. Topics may include...
[MB360](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB360)Laboratory in Molecular Microbiology A laboratory-intensive course with supplementary discussions and lectures. Topics include experimental design and analysis, genetic manipulation of bacterial plasmids and chromosomes, bioinformatical analysis of bacterial genomes, and physiological...
[MB365](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB365)Laboratory in Genomics This laboratory-intensive course provides a hands-on introduction to asking biological questions at the whole-genome level. Students gain experience preparing samples for high-throughput sequencing and analyzing the data.
[MB375](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB375)Laboratory in Advanced Cell Biology Laboratory-intensive, inquiry-based course that focuses on techniques in cell biology, with an emphasis on understanding the cell biology. Modern molecular biology techniques may also be employed. Course emphasizes experimental design, techniques, da...
[MB397](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB397)Mentored Research in Molecular Biology Alternative Format Mentored research projects based on laboratory investigation, using techniques or concepts introduced ingenetics. Laboratory intensive. Taught as an extended format course over four blocks, or over half block.
[MB399](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB399)Mentored Research in Molecular Biology Mentored research projects based on laboratory investigation, using techniques or concepts introduced in genetics. Laboratory intensive
[MB401](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB401)Advanced Special Topics in Molecular Biology Special topics not offered on a regular basis. Lecture/Discussion.
[MB405](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB405)Stem Cell Biology Stem cells are undifferentiated cells that are capable of self-renewal and have the potential to develop into specialized cells types. Stem cells are important for development, reproduction, growth, healing, and homeostasis. Course covers the microen...
[MB410](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB410)Molecular and Cellular Virology The molecular genetics, cell biology, and biochemistry of viruses, including attachment to, entry into, and synthesis and assembly inside of host cells. Through lecture and discussion, the course emphasizes the experimental basis of biologists’ under...
[MB415](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB415)Developmental Neurobiology Contemporary approaches to the study of nervous system development with emphasis on molecular and genetic techniques. Topics include neural induction, neural stem cells, axon guidance, synapse formation, neuronal life and death, and other topics. Rea...
[MB425](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB425)Molecular and Cellular Biology of Cancer In his 1966 Nobel lecture, Peyton Rous described cancer as follows: “Tumours destroy man in a unique and appalling way, as flesh of his own flesh which has somehow been rendered proliferative, rampant, predatory, and ungovernable. They are the most c...
[MB430](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB430)Sex Differentiation Molecular and cellular basis for sex determination and differentiation, with an emphasis on evolution and diversity in sexual systems across the tree of life. Course content includes scientific discoveries in the field and their historical context fr...
[MB450](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB450)Advanced Special Topics in Laboratory Research in Molecular Biology Laboratory-intensive special topics not offered on a regular basis. Laboratory intensive.
[MB497](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB497)Senior Capstone in Molecular Biology Seminar-style course exploring not only the science related to a topic in molecular biology but also related social issues. Topic will be selected by faculty on an annual basis and announced to majors before pre-registration. Examples of possible top...
[MB498](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB498)Advanced Mentored Research in Molecular Biology Advanced mentored research projects based on laboratory investigation, using techniques or concepts introduced in genetics. Laboratory intensive.
[MB499](https://coursecatalog.coloradocollege.edu/courses/MB499)Senior Thesis in Molecular Biology Writing of the senior thesis based on data from an undergraduate research project.
